summary: Cut the Workbench route and verification contract over to local actor ownership without admin/password login.
rationale: This PR changes the Workbench BFF and the dev Workbench verification script together so the PR remains independently testable after the /api/auth routes are removed. The product contract is intentionally breaking: the host environment and LLM key provide access, not a Workbench admin/password session.
verification: PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 uv run --group dev python -m pytest tests/test_workbench_static_frontend.py tests/test_react_workbench_cutover_gate.py -q -p no:cacheprovider
PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 uv run --group dev python -m pytest tests/test_agent_workbench_contract.py -q -p no:cacheprovider
CI=1 pnpm --dir apps/web-react exec vitest run src/lib/stream/agentStream.test.ts src/lib/stream/agentStreamReducer.test.ts src/lib/stream/agentStreamView.test.ts --run
uv run --group dev python tools/check_pr_governance.py --base main
deletion_targets: Workbench admin/password route dependency from the dev Workbench smoke path
Workbench session cookie and CSRF dependency from frontend-to-BFF requests
legacy auth security test coverage replaced by local actor route coverage
risks: Workbench BFF routes must remain owner-scoped to the local actor after session cookies are removed.
The dev Workbench verification script must exercise the real backend without reintroducing auth fallback.
Generated OpenAPI types must match the auth-free route contract.
